title: Refurbished / grant-funded scanner — capex provenance gap
description: African teaching hospital with 1 scanner acquired at 40% of list price (refurbished or donor-funded) with a 5-year service contract bundled in. Currently the calculator accepts the lowered scanner_cost directly — which is correct arithmetically — but there's no provenance tracking on whether the number is real or a dream. No warranty/AMC structure modeled, no end-of-life replacement scheduled.

expected.notes: EXPECTED OUTCOME: calculator runs with a much better NPV because capex is 40% of normal. The finding is that there's no data-hygiene affordance for 'is this scanner price real or aspirational?' and no service-contract structure. Outcome: capex-mode enum (new / refurbished / grant / subscription) with each producing different warranty + replacement-schedule lifecycle costs.
